name: Q2302257
abstract: Owen Joseph "Sonny" Igoe (October 8, 1923 – March 28, 2012) was an American jazz drummer and music educator who, toured with the orchestras of Tommy Reed (1913–2012), Les Elgart, Ina Ray Hutton, Benny Goodman, and Woody Herman from the mid-1940s to the mid-1950s. From the mid-1940s to 1988, he performed on over 79 recordings with bands a…

interviews.title: Sonny Igoe interviewed by Monk Rowe, Emerson, New Jersey, July 31, 2003

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Pee Wee Erwin All-Stars

works.t: I Thought I Heard Buddy Bolden Say

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: I said, 'man, I'd really like to study with you.' ... So he took me on ... he put the thing down and he said, 'I can see you're a swinger.' ... It was a breakthrough.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: And Red was the bass player and then Sonny Igoe on drums, and it was a good experience and I enjoyed it very much.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I know I worked with Sonny Igoe who played with Benny a long time
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]
